title: On the Sovereignty of Self
author: From: The Journals of Ralph Waldo Emerson
content: A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds, adored by little statesmen and philosophers and divines. With consistency a great soul has simply nothing to do.

title: The Diderot Effect
author: From: Denis Diderot
content: A new possession often spirals into a need for more, leading to a loss of focus and an increase in complexity. You do not need to upgrade your tools to upgrade your work.

title: The Pygmalion Effect (Refined)
author: From: Rosenthal and Jacobson
content: Others perform according to the expectations we project onto them. When we set the bar low, we guarantee failure.

title: The Planning Fallacy
author: From: Daniel Kahneman
content: We underestimate the time needed to complete a task, even when we have data showing it always takes longer than we think.

title: The Anchoring Bias
author: From: Behavioral Economics
content: The first piece of information you hear often dictates your entire decision-making process. Don't let the first number you see be the only one you consider.
